title: Room reservations — interval-overlap correctness
run_contract: The produced project MUST include an executable `start.sh` at the repo root that launches the service IN THE FOREGROUND on the port from the PORT env var (PORT=0 = any free port), binds 127.0.0.1, and prints exactly `LISTENING <port>` once bound. HTTP with JSON bodies. The agent sees the per-stage prose + this contract only — never the conformance suite or invariant check.

named_invariant.statement: On any room, no two ACTIVE reservations overlap. Intervals are half-open [start,end): two intervals [a,b) and [c,d) overlap iff a < d AND c < b (adjacent intervals sharing an endpoint do NOT overlap). Cancelled reservations free their interval.

notes: Rung 3 of the discriminator hunt: interval-overlap is a classic source of subtle boundary bugs (<= vs <). A one-shot impl frequently mishandles adjacency or containment; the arm that writes+runs tests should catch it.
